_comment: TEST-ONLY mechanistic answer key for the PRD mechanistic-reasoning eval (PRD lines 77-84). Hand-reviewed. The scorer in test_agent.py compares the model's submit_recommendation.mechanism against this. Empty lists are meaningful: they encode a correct-negative (e.g. 'no transporter'), which the model must also get right rather than inventing one. protein_binding_percent is scored within an absolute tolerance. Verify against label/DrugBank/PubMed before any real use.

midazolam.source: Label / DrugBank — CYP3A4/3A5, P-gp substrate; 1-hydroxymidazolam active (minor).

vancomycin.source: Label — ~80-90% renal unchanged; no CYP; no active metabolite.

morphine.source: DrugBank — UGT2B7 glucuronidation; M6G active (renally cleared), M3G neuro-excitatory; P-gp substrate.

gentamicin.source: Label — glomerular filtration, unchanged; not metabolised; low protein binding.

amikacin.source: Label — renal filtration, unchanged; not metabolised; low protein binding.

fentanyl.source: DrugBank — CYP3A4 to inactive norfentanyl; P-gp substrate; no active metabolite.

ampicillin.source: Label — predominantly renal (tubular secretion + filtration), minimal metabolism.

clindamycin.source: DrugBank — hepatic CYP3A4 (to N-demethyl/sulfoxide); high protein binding.
